key_insight: The paper accelerates phase-field cohesive-zone fracture modeling to predict mixed-mode fracture and high-cycle fatigue in UHPC and HSS without simulating every load cycle.
engineering_relevance: It provides a more practical computational tool for assessing durability and failure risk in cyclic-loaded infrastructure made from high-performance materials.
method: Derive a generic stress-based failure criterion for UHPC from biaxial tensile tests.
Embed a fatigue degradation law into the PF-CZM fracture framework.
Use an envelope-load approximation plus adaptive cyclic increments to accelerate high-cycle fatigue simulation.
evidence_signal: The model is validated against relevant experimental comparisons and reproduces mixed-mode fracture and high-cycle fatigue behavior for both UHPC and HSS.
watch_item: The acceleration and adaptive-cycle assumptions may need careful calibration to ensure accuracy across different loading histories and material systems.
paper_title: High performance construction materials fracture and high cycle fatigue assessment based on accelerated PF-CZM
